task_context.actionable_question: Which assessment is causing the most students to fail?
task_context.ai_prompt_hint: Flag assessments with pass_flag=False rate above threshold. Suggest curriculum review.

explanation.raw_text:
Summary: The analysis of assessment difficulty reveals that the G1 assessment has the highest fail rate at 7.7%, indicating it is the most challenging for students. In contrast, G3 shows the lowest fail rate at 4.6%, suggesting it is more manageable for the cohort.

Insights: High Fail Rate in G1: G1 has a fail rate of 7.7%, which is significantly higher than the other assessments, indicating that it poses the greatest challenge to students. | Lower Fail Rates in G2 and G3: G2 and G3 have lower fail rates of 5.1% and 4.6%, respectively, suggesting that students are performing better in these assessments.

Educational implications: The high fail rate in G1 suggests a need for targeted interventions to support students in this competency area.

Recommendations: high - Review the curriculum and instructional strategies for G1. - To address the high fail rate and improve student understanding and performance.

required_core_outputs.description: Flag assessments with pass_flag=False rate above threshold.

evaluation_constraints.description: Frame a high fail rate as a signal for assessment or curriculum review, not as evidence of student deficiency.
Avoid causal claims about student quality.

evaluation_requirements.safety_fairness_note: Retained as applicable because curriculum recommendations can affect learners; candidate for human review rather than automatic exclusion.
